findings: Destination Port distribution shows strong class-conditioned skew with DDoS class dominated by a single port and zero entropy.
BENIGN class exhibits higher entropy and more diverse port distribution.
Distribution metrics are stable across time segments within evidence e33.
The Destination Port feature within the DDoS class exhibits near-zero entropy and a dominant port value, confirming strong class-conditioned distribution skew.
No additional cardinality anomalies were detected that would undermine the observed distributional pattern.
Destination Port feature exhibits a moderate shortcut-like predictive signal for DDoS class samples in evidence e33, consistent with strong class-conditioned distributional skew.
DDoS class samples are dominated by a single destination port with zero entropy, contrasting with higher entropy and lower dominant ratio in BENIGN class samples.
limitations: Shortcut verification used a deterministic one-feature decision stump, which may not capture more complex dependencies or interactions.
